channel.title: Middleware on Ravikanth Chaganti
channel.description: Recent content in Middleware on Ravikanth Chaganti

item.title: Middleware in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: By the time we have function tools, streaming, and a Foundry-backed agent in place, most of the interesting logic still lives in the agent itself: the system prompt, the tools, and the conversation. That is fine for a sample, but production agents quickly grow a second category of concerns that do not really belong in the prompt or the tools, things like logging every run, redacting PII before it leaves the process, blocking obvious abuse, timing function calls, retrying transient model errors, or rewriting the final response. Putting any of that into the agent definition makes the agent harder to read and harder to reuse.
